name: aggregation
context: Build MongoDB aggregation pipelines for reporting, reshaping, joins, and materialized outputs
info: Aggregation is MongoDB’s pipeline engine for transformations that exceed plain find queries. This skill covers stage ordering, common stages such as `$match`, `$project`, `$group`, `$unwind`, `$lookup`, and `$sort`, and output stages like `$merge`, plus practical optimization rules such as moving `$match` early, exploiting indexes, and using `allowDiskUse` when the pipeline would exceed in-memory limits. Agents use it for analytics, denormalized views, exports, and repair or enrichment jobs.
body:
# Aggregation Pipelines

Use aggregation when a plain `find()` is no longer expressive enough. A pipeline passes documents through ordered stages that filter, reshape, join, group, sort, or materialize results.

```javascript
const pipeline = [
  { $match: { status: "paid", createdAt: { $gte: start } } },
  { $unwind: "$lineItems" },
  { $group: { _id: "$lineItems.sku", revenue: { $sum: "$lineItems.total" }, orders: { $sum: 1 } } },
  { $sort: { revenue: -1 } },
  { $limit: 20 }
];

const results = await db.collection("orders").aggregate(pipeline, { allowDiskUse: true }).toArray();
```

## Common Stage Roles

- `$match` — filter early so later stages process less data
- `$project` — keep only the fields the next stages need
- `$group` — aggregate by key with accumulators such as `$sum`, `$avg`, `$first`, `$last`
- `$lookup` — join data from another collection
- `$merge` / `$out` — write results to a collection; treat these as destructive or materializing operations

Use `explain()` on the pipeline when it is slow. Early `$match` and `$sort` stages can use indexes, especially when they remain near the front of the pipeline. See [[mongodb/indexes]] for the follow-up workflow.

sections.title: Stage order matters
sections.body: Put `$match`, `$limit`, and `$skip` as early as semantics allow so fewer documents enter expensive stages. MongoDB can use indexes for early `$match` and, in some cases, early `$sort`. If you place `$project`, `$group`, or `$unwind` too early, you can block index use and inflate the amount of work downstream.

sections.title: allowDiskUse and memory pressure
sections.body: Some pipelines exceed in-memory limits, especially when they sort or group large working sets. In those cases pass `allowDiskUse: true` explicitly and still tighten the pipeline because disk-backed execution is slower than an indexed, selective pipeline. `allowDiskUse` is a relief valve, not a substitute for query design.

sections.title: Materialized outputs
sections.body: Use `$merge` to write results into an existing collection with controlled merge behavior, or `$out` to replace the destination collection wholesale. These stages change data, so agents should treat them like write operations and confirm the target collection, write concern, and desired idempotency before running them.

links.context: Aggregation still depends on the correct URI, role, and topology
Tune pipelines by adding indexes that support early match and sort stages
Use plain finds when a pipeline would be unnecessary complexity

related.note: Aggregation pipelines run against a live deployment and need the correct roles and connection settings
Pipeline performance often depends on supporting indexes for early stages
General index design principles still apply when tuning aggregation workloads

scripts.name: Run aggregation pipeline
scripts.description: Execute an aggregation pipeline with optional explain or allowDiskUse settings using Extended JSON input

AGGREGATION_INPUT_JSON.description: Extended JSON payload. If omitted, the script reads JSON from stdin.

options.description: Aggregate options such as allowDiskUse, maxTimeMS, or hint

explain.description: Return an explain plan instead of results

count.description: Number of returned result documents when not using explain

depends.reason: The pipeline runs through the live deployment and still needs correct connection and role settings
